name: Superpower Stuffed Poblano Peppers

ingredients.item: cooked black beans, drained and rinsed
chipotle pepper in adobo sauce, chopped small, and 1 tablespoon adobo sauce
raw cashews, soaked from 1 hour to overnight and drained

method: Place the peppers on a baking sheet and set on the top shelf of the broiler. Turn often with tongs to get all sides blackened. Let the peppers cool and then rub off the blackened skin with a damp paper towel or peel with a paring knife. Just grab an edge of the skin with the knife and peel off. Be careful not to tear up the peppers, since they will be stuffed. They will probably split naturally at one spot so use that as your opening to remove the seeds. Set aside.

Rinse the quinoa and place in a large saucepan. Pour in the vegetable broth and bring to a boil. Cover and turn down heat to medium and cook for about 15 minutes or until all the liquid is absorbed. Remove from the heat and set aside with the cover on.
Heat the oil in a small skillet over medium heat. Add the onion and cook 10 to 15 minutes or until translucent. Stir in the garlic and cook for 1 more minute.

Add the quinoa, beans, corn, chili pepper, extra adobo sauce, chili powder, cumin, and salt to a large bowl. Mix well. Stuff each pepper with ¼ of the stuffing and place in a casserole that will hold them snugly. Bake for 15 to 20 minutes.

Add all the ingredients to a blender and blend until smooth. Turn this blended mixture into a saucepan. Cook on medium heat and stir until the sauce thickens a bit. It will take about 5 to 10 minutes.
Remove the stuffed peppers from the oven and drizzle cheese sauce over everything.
